description: Numbered-lemma blueprint of a strong Prime Number Theorem proof; proof-DAG subset (foundational lemmas 1-120). Dependencies parsed from explicit in-proof citations.
note: Subset of a larger blueprint; nodes keyed L/D/T + number.

L1.statement: For 𝑡 > 1 we have 2log 𝑡 ≤ 𝑂(log 𝑡)
L1.title: Log growth

L2.statement: For 𝑡 ≥ 2 we have log(𝑡2) = 2log 𝑡
L2.title: Square log

L4.statement: For 𝑡 ≥ 2 we have log(2𝑡) ≤ 2log 𝑡
L4.title: Log compare

L5.statement: For any 𝑛 ≥ 1 and 𝛼, 𝛽 ∈ ℂ we have 𝑛𝛼+𝛽 = 𝑛𝛼 ⋅ 𝑛𝛽
L5.title: Exp rule

L6.statement: For 𝑏 ∈ ℝ and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ we have ℜ(𝑏𝑤) = 𝑏ℜ(𝑤).
L6.title: Real scale

L7.statement: For a convergent series 𝑣 = ∑ ∞ 𝑛=1 𝑣𝑛 with 𝑣𝑛 ∈ ℂ , we have ℜ(𝑣) = ∑ ∞ 𝑛=1 ℜ(𝑣𝑛).
L7.title: Real series

L8.statement: For 𝑎 ∈ ℝ we have 𝑒𝑖𝑎 = cos(𝑎) + 𝑖sin(𝑎)
L8.title: Euler’s formula

L9.statement: For 𝑎 ∈ ℝ we have ℜ(𝑒𝑖𝑎) =cos(𝑎)
L9.title: Real cosine

L10.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1 we have 𝑛 = 𝑒log 𝑛.
L10.title: Log inverse

L11.statement: For 𝑎 ∈ ℝ we have cos(−𝑎) =cos(𝑎)
L11.title: Cos even

L12.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1, 𝑦 ∈ ℝ we have cos(−𝑦log 𝑛) =cos(𝑦log 𝑛)
L12.title: Cos even

L13.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1 and 𝑦 ∈ ℝ we have 𝑛−𝑖𝑦 = 𝑒−𝑖𝑦 log 𝑛.
L13.title: Exp form

L14.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1 and 𝑦 ∈ ℝ we have ℜ(𝑒−𝑖𝑦 log 𝑛) =cos(−𝑦log 𝑛).
L14.title: Real cosine

L15.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1 and 𝑦 ∈ ℝ we have ℜ(𝑛−𝑖𝑦) =cos(−𝑦log 𝑛).
L15.title: Real cosine

L16.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1 and 𝑦 ∈ ℝ we have ℜ(𝑛−𝑖𝑦) =cos(𝑦log 𝑛).
L16.title: Real cosine

L17.statement: For any 𝜃 ∈ ℝ we have cos(2𝜃) = 2cos(𝜃)2 − 1.
L17.title: Double angle

L18.statement: For any 𝜃 ∈ ℝ we have 2cos(𝜃)2 = 1 + cos(2𝜃).
L18.title: Cos square

L19.statement: For any 𝜃 ∈ ℝ we have 2(1 +cos(𝜃))2 = 2 + 4 cos(𝜃) + 2cos(𝜃)2.
L19.title: Square expand

L20.statement: For any 𝜃 ∈ ℝ we have 2(1 +cos(𝜃))2 = 3 + 4 cos(𝜃) +cos(2𝜃).
L20.title: Trig identity

L21.statement: For 𝑦 ∈ ℝ we have 0 ≤ 𝑦2.
L21.title: Square nonneg

L23.statement: For any 𝜃 ∈ ℝ we have 0 ≤ 2(1 +cos(𝜃))2.
L23.title: Cos square

L24.statement: For any 𝜃 ∈ ℝ we have 0 ≤ 3 + 4 cos(𝜃) +cos(2𝜃).
L24.title: Trig positive

L25.statement: For 𝑛 ≥ 1 and 𝑡 ∈ ℝ we have 0 ≤ 3 + 4 cos(𝑡log 𝑛) +cos(2𝑡log 𝑛).
L25.title: Trig positive

L26.statement: For a convergent series 𝑟 = ∑ ∞ 𝑛=1 𝑟𝑛, if 𝑟𝑛 ≥ 0 for all 𝑛 ≥ 1, then 𝑟 ≥ 0.
L26.title: Series positive

L27.statement: For any 𝑤 ∈ ℂ, ℜ(2𝑀 − 𝑤) = 2𝑀 − ℜ(𝑤).
L27.title: Real part diff

L29.statement: For 𝑥, 𝑀 ∈ ℝ, if 𝑥 ≤ 𝑀 then 2𝑀 − 𝑥 ≥ 𝑀.
L29.title: Inequality reversal

L30.statement: For 𝑤 ∈ ℂ and 𝑀 > 0, if ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then 2𝑀 −ℜ(𝑤) ≥ 𝑀.
L30.title: Real part lower bound

L31.statement: For 𝑤 ∈ ℂ and 𝑀 > 0, if ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then ℜ(2𝑀 − 𝑤) ≥ 𝑀.
L31.title: Real part bound

L32.statement: For 𝑤 ∈ ℂ and 𝑀 > 0, if ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then ℜ(2𝑀 − 𝑤) > 0.
L32.title: Real part >0

L33.statement: If 𝑤 ∈ ℂ has ℜ(𝑤) > 0, then 𝑤 ≠ 0.
L33.title: Pos real nonzero

L34.statement: For 𝑤 ∈ ℂ and 𝑀 > 0, if ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then 2𝑀 − 𝑤 ≠ 0.
L34.title: 2M minus nonzero

L35.statement: Let 𝑧 ∈ ℂ . If 𝑧 ≠ 0 then |𝑧| > 0.
L35.title: Absolute value positive

L36.statement: For 𝑤 ∈ ℂ and 𝑀 > 0, if ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then |2𝑀 − 𝑤| > 0.
L36.title: 2M minus mod

L37.statement: For any 𝑤 ∈ ℂ, we have 𝑤 = ℜ(𝑤) + 𝑖ℑ𝑤.
L37.title: Real imaginary

L38.statement: For any 𝑎, 𝑏 ∈ ℝ, we have |𝑎 + 𝑖𝑏|2 = 𝑎2 + 𝑏2.
L38.title: Mod square

L39.statement: For any 𝑎, 𝑏, 𝑐 ∈ ℝ, we have |𝑐 − 𝑎 − 𝑖𝑏|2 = (𝑐 − 𝑎)2 + 𝑏2.
L39.title: Shifted mod

L40.statement: For any 𝑎, 𝑏, 𝑐 ∈ ℝ, we have |𝑐 − 𝑎 − 𝑖𝑏|2 − |𝑎 + 𝑖𝑏|2 = (𝑐 − 𝑎)2 − 𝑎2.
L40.title: Mod diff

L41.statement: For any 𝑎, 𝑐 ∈ ℝ, we have (𝑐 − 𝑎)2 = 𝑎2 − 2𝑎𝑐 + 𝑐2.
L41.title: Square expand

L42.statement: For any 𝑎, 𝑐 ∈ ℝ, we have (𝑐 − 𝑎)2 − 𝑎2 = 2𝑐(𝑐 − 𝑎).
L42.title: Square diff

L43.statement: For any 𝑎, 𝑏, 𝑐 ∈ ℝ, we have |𝑐 − 𝑎 − 𝑖𝑏|2 − |𝑎 + 𝑖𝑏|2 = 2𝑐(𝑐 − 𝑎).
L43.title: Mod diff

L44.statement: For any 𝑤 ∈ ℂ, |2𝑀 − ℜ(𝑤) − 𝑖ℑ𝑤|2 − |ℜ(𝑤) + 𝑖ℑ𝑤|2 = 4𝑀 (𝑀 − ℜ(𝑤)).
L44.title: Modulus diff

L45.statement: For any 𝑤 ∈ ℂ, |2𝑀 − 𝑤|2 − |𝑤|2 = 4𝑀 (𝑀 − ℜ(𝑤)).
L45.title: Modulus identity

L46.statement: If 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑥 ≤ 𝑀, then 4𝑀 (𝑀 − 𝑥) ≥ 0.
L46.title: Nonneg product

L47.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ. If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then 4𝑀 (𝑀 − ℜ(𝑤)) ≥ 0.
L47.title: Nonneg product

L48.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ. If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then |2𝑀 −𝑤|2 −|𝑤|2 ≥ 0.
L48.title: Modulus compare

L49.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ. If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then |2𝑀 − 𝑤|2 ≥ |𝑤|2.
L49.title: Modulus bound

L50.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ. If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then |2𝑀 − 𝑤| ≥ |𝑤|.
L50.title: Modulus bound

L51.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ. If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then |𝑤| ≤ |2𝑀 − 𝑤|.
L51.title: Modulus order

L52.statement: If 𝑐 > 0 and 0 ≤ 𝑎 ≤ 𝑏, then 𝑎/𝑐 ≤ 𝑏/𝑐.
L52.title: Divide inequality

L53.statement: If 𝑏 > 0 and 0 ≤ 𝑎 ≤ 𝑏, then 𝑎/𝑏 ≤ 1.
L53.title: Ratio bound

L54.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ . If |2𝑀 − 𝑤| > 0 and |𝑤| ≤ |2𝑀 − 𝑤| then |𝑤| |2𝑀−𝑤| ≤ 1.
L54.title: Ratio bound

L55.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ . If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀and |𝑤| ≤ |2𝑀 − 𝑤| then |𝑤| |2𝑀−𝑤| ≤ 1.
L55.title: Ratio bound

L56.statement: Let 𝑀 > 0 and 𝑤 ∈ ℂ. If ℜ(𝑤) ≤ 𝑀then |𝑤| |2𝑀−𝑤| ≤ 1.
L56.title: Ratio bound

L57.statement: Let 𝑁 , 𝐺 ∈ ℂ. We have |𝑁 + 𝐺| ≤ |𝑁 | + |𝐺|
L57.title: Triangle inequality

L58.statement: Let 𝑁 , 𝐹 ∈ ℂ. We have |𝑁 − 𝐹 | ≤ |𝑁 | + |𝐹 |
L58.title: Triangle minus

L59.statement: Let 𝑟 > 0 and 𝑁 , 𝐹 ∈ ℂ. We have 𝑟|𝑁 − 𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟(|𝑁 | + |𝐹 |)
L59.title: Scaled triangle

L60.statement: Let 𝑟 > 0 and 𝑁 , 𝐹 ∈ ℂ. We have 𝑟|𝑁 − 𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟|𝑁 | + 𝑟|𝐹 |
L60.title: Scaled triangle

L61.statement: Let 0 < 𝑟 < 𝑅 and 𝑁 , 𝐹 ∈ ℂ. If 𝑅|𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟|𝑁 −𝐹 |then 𝑅|𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟|𝑁 |+𝑟|𝐹 |
L61.title: Ineq step

L62.statement: Let 0 < 𝑟 < 𝑅 and 𝑁 , 𝐹 ∈ ℂ . If 𝑅|𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟|𝑁 − 𝐹 | then (𝑅 − 𝑟)|𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟|𝑁 |
L62.title: Rearranged bound

L63.statement: For 𝑎 ∈ ℝ, if 𝑎 > 0 then |𝑎| = 𝑎.
L63.title: Abs positive

L64.statement: For 𝑎 ∈ ℝ, if 𝑎 > 0 then 2𝑎 > 0.
L64.title: Double positive

L65.statement: For 𝑎 ∈ ℝ, if 𝑎 > 0 then |2𝑎| = 2𝑎.
L65.title: Scaled abs

L66.statement: Let 0 < 𝑟 < 𝑅 , 𝑀 > 0 , and 𝐹 ∈ ℂ . If 𝑅𝐹 ≤ 𝑟|2𝑀 − 𝐹 | then (𝑅 − 𝑟)|𝐹 | ≤ 2𝑀 𝑟
L66.title: Key bound

L67.statement: Let 0 < 𝑟 < 𝑅 and 𝐹 ∈ ℂ . Then we have (𝑅 − 𝑟)|𝐹 | ≥ 0
L67.title: Nonneg factor

L68.statement: Let 0 < 𝑟 < 𝑅 , 𝑀 > 0 , and 𝐹 ∈ ℂ . If (𝑅 − 𝑟)|𝐹 | ≤ 2𝑀 𝑟then |𝐹 | ≤ 2𝑀𝑟 𝑅−𝑟 . 5
L68.title: Divide bound

L69.statement: Let 0 < 𝑟 < 𝑅 , 𝑀 > 0 , and 𝐹 ∈ ℂ . If 𝑅|𝐹 | ≤ 𝑟|2𝑀 − 𝐹 | then |𝐹 | ≤ 2𝑀𝑟 𝑅−𝑟
L69.title: Final bound

L70.statement: Let 𝑓 ∶ ℂ → ℂ be analytic, and let 𝑛0 be the analyticOrderAt for 𝑓 at 0. If 𝑓(0) = 0then 𝑛0 ≠ 0.
L70.title: Order nonzero

L71.statement: Let 𝑓 ∶ ℂ → ℂ be analytic, and let 𝑛0 be the analyticOrderAt for 𝑓 at 0. If 𝑓 ≠ 0 then 𝑛0 ∈ ℕ.
L71.title: Order natural

L72.statement: Let 𝑓 ∶ ℂ → ℂ be analytic at 0, and let 𝑛0 be the analyticOrderAt for 𝑓 at 0. If 𝑛0 ∈ ℕ then there exists a nhd 𝑁 of 0 and 𝑔 ∶ ℂ → ℂ such that 𝑔 is analytic at 0, and 𝑓(𝑧) = 𝑧𝑛0 𝑔(𝑧)on 𝑁.
L72.title: Factor power

L73.statement: Let 𝑓 ∶ ℂ → ℂ be analytic at 0, and let 𝑛0 be the analyticOrderAt for 𝑓 at 0. If 𝑛0 ∈ ℕ and 𝑛0 ≠ 0, then there exists a nhd 𝑁 of 0 and ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ such that ℎ is analytic at 0, and 𝑓(𝑧) = 𝑧ℎ(𝑧)on 𝑁.
L73.title: Factor linear

L74.statement: Let 𝑓 ∶ ℂ → ℂ be analytic at 0, and let 𝑛0 be the analyticOrderAt for 𝑓 at 0. If 𝑓 ≠ 0 and 𝑓(0) = 0, then ℎ(𝑧) = 𝑓(𝑧)/𝑧is analytic at 0.
L74.title: Divide zero

L75.statement: The function 𝑓1(𝑧) =1 𝑧 is analytic on {𝑧 ∈ ℂ ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0}.
L75.title: Inverse analytic

L76.statement: Let 𝑇 ⊂ 𝑆 ⊂ ℂ and 𝑓 ∶ 𝑆 → ℂ . If 𝑓 is analytic on 𝑆 then 𝑓 is analytic on 𝑇 .
L76.title: Analytic mono

L77.statement: Let 0 < 𝑅 < 1 and 𝑉 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} and 𝑈 = {𝑧 ∈ ℂ ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0}. Then 𝑉 ⊂ 𝑈 .
L77.title: Nonzero subset

L78.statement: The function 𝑓1(𝑧) =1 𝑧 is analytic on 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} .
L78.title: Inverse analytic

L79.statement: Let 𝑇 ⊂ 𝑆 ⊂ ℂ , and let 𝑓1 ∶ 𝑆 → ℂ and 𝑓2 ∶ 𝑆 → ℂ . If 𝑓1 is analytic on 𝑇 and 𝑓2 is analytic on 𝑇 , then 𝑓1 ⋅ 𝑓2 is analytic on 𝑇 .
L79.title: Product analytic

L80.statement: Let 𝑇 ⊂ 𝑆 ⊂ ℂ , and let 𝑓1 ∶ 𝑆 → ℂ and 𝑓2 ∶ 𝑆 → ℂ . If 𝑓1 is analytic on 𝑇 and 𝑓2 is analytic on 𝑆, then 𝑓1 ⋅ 𝑓2 is analytic on 𝑇 .
L80.title: Product analytic

L81.statement: Let 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} and 𝑓1 ∶ ℂ → ℂ and 𝑓2 ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If 𝑓1 is analytic on 𝑇 and 𝑓2 is analytic on 𝔻𝑅, then 𝑓1 ⋅ 𝑓2 is analytic on 𝑇 .
L81.title: Product analytic

L82.statement: Let 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} and 𝑓 ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If 𝑓(𝑧)is analytic on 𝔻𝑅, then 𝑓(𝑧)/𝑧is analytic on 𝑇 .
L82.title: Quotient analytic

L83.statement: Let 𝑉 ⊂ ℂ and ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If ℎ is AnalyticOn 𝑉 , then ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 𝑧 for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝑉 .
L83.title: On implies within

L84.statement: Let ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 𝑧 for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅, then ℎ is AnalyticOn 𝔻𝑅.
L84.title: Within implies on

L85.statement: Let 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} . Then 𝔻𝑅 = {0} ∪ 𝑇.
L85.title: Disk split

L86.statement: Let 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} and ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 0 and ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 𝑧 for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝑇 , then ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 𝑧 for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅.
L86.title: Within union

L87.statement: Let 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} and ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If ℎ is AnalyticWithi- nAt 0 and ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 𝑧 for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝑇 , then ℎ is AnalyticOn 𝔻𝑅.
L87.title: Within gives on

L88.statement: Let ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If ℎ is AnalyticAt 0, then ℎ is AnalyticWithinAt 0.
L88.title: At to within

L89.statement: Let 𝑇 = {𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 ∶ 𝑧 ≠ 0} and ℎ ∶ ℂ → ℂ . If ℎ is AnalyticAt 0 and ℎ is AnalyticOn 𝑇 , then ℎ is AnalyticOn 𝔻𝑅.
L89.title: Local to global

D90.statement: For 𝑅 > 0, define the open ball 𝔻𝑅 ∶= {𝑧 ∈ ℂ ∶ |𝑧| < 𝑅}.
D90.title: Open disk

L91.statement: For 𝑅 > 0, the closure of 𝔻𝑅 equals 𝔻𝑅 ∶= {𝑧 ∈ ℂ ∶ |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅}
L91.title: Disk closure

L92.statement: For 𝑅 > 0, if 𝑤 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 then |𝑤| ≤ 𝑅.
L92.title: In disk bound

L93.statement: For 𝑅 > 0, if 𝑤 ∉ 𝔻𝑅 then |𝑤| ≥ 𝑅.
L93.title: Outside disk bound

L94.statement: For 𝑅 > 0, if |𝑤| ≤ 𝑅 and |𝑤| ≥ 𝑅 then |𝑤| = 𝑅.
L94.title: Modulus equal

L95.statement: For 𝑅 > 0, if 𝑤 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 and 𝑤 ∉ 𝔻𝑅, then |𝑤| = 𝑅.
L95.title: Boundary modulus

L98.statement: For 𝑅 > 0 we have 𝑅 ∈ 𝔻𝑅.
L98.title: Positive radius belongs to its closed disk

L99.statement: For 𝑅 > 0 the ball 𝔻𝑅 is a compact subset of ℂ.
L99.title: Compactness

L100.statement: If 𝐾 ⊂ ℂ is compact and 𝑔 ∶ 𝐾 → ℂ is continuous, then there exists 𝑣 ∈ 𝐾 such that |𝑔(𝑣)| ≥ |𝑔(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝐾.
L100.title: ExtrValThm

L101.statement: If 𝑔 ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ is continuous, then there exists 𝑣 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 such that |𝑔(𝑣)| ≥ |𝑔(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅.
L101.title: Disk Boundary

L102.statement: If ℎ ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ is analytic, then ℎ is continuous.
L102.title: Analytic Continuation

L103.statement: If ℎ ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ is analytic, then there exists 𝑢 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑢)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅.
L103.title: Max modulus

L104.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 . Let ℎ ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ be analytic. Suppose there exists 𝑤 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑤)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻 𝑅. Then |ℎ(𝑧)| = |ℎ(𝑤)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅.
L104.title: Interior max

L105.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 . Let ℎ ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ be analytic. Suppose there exists 𝑤 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑤)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻 𝑅. Then |ℎ(𝑅)| = |ℎ(𝑤)|.
L105.title: Boundary value

L106.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0. Let ℎ ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ be analytic. Suppose there exists 𝑤 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑤)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻 𝑅. Then |ℎ(𝑅)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅
L106.title: Boundary bound

L107.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0. Let ℎ ∶ 𝔻𝑅 → ℂ be analytic. There exists 𝑣 ∈ 𝔻𝑅 with |𝑣| = 𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑣)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ 𝔻𝑅
L107.title: Boundary point

L108.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0. Let ℎ(𝑧)be analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅. There exists 𝑣 ∈ ℂ with |𝑣| = 𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑣)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑧)|for all 𝑧 ∈ ℂ with |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅.
L108.title: Boundary point

L109.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 and 𝐵 ≥ 0. Let ℎ(𝑧)be a function analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅. Suppose that |ℎ(𝑧)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑧 ∈ ℂ with |𝑧| = 𝑅 . Then there exists 𝑣 ∈ ℂ with |𝑣| = 𝑅 such that |ℎ(𝑣)| ≥ |ℎ(𝑤)|for all 𝑤 ∈ ℂ with |𝑤| ≤ 𝑅, and |ℎ(𝑣)| ≤ 𝐵.
L109.title: Boundary max

L110.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 and 𝐵 ≥ 0. Let ℎ(𝑧)be a function analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅. Suppose that |ℎ(𝑧)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑧 ∈ ℂ with |𝑧| = 𝑅. Then |ℎ(𝑤)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑤 ∈ ℂ with |𝑤| ≤ 𝑅.
L110.title: Max principle

L111.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 and 𝐵 ≥ 0. Let ℎ(𝑧)be a function analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅. Suppose that |ℎ(𝑤)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑤 ∈ ℂ with |𝑤| ≤ 𝑅. Then |ℎ(𝑧)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑧 ∈ ℂ with |𝑧| = 𝑅.
L111.title: Easy maximum principle

L112.statement: Let 𝑇 > 0 and 𝐵 ≥ 0 . Let ℎ(𝑧)be a function analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑇 . We have |ℎ(𝑧)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑧 ∈ ℂ with |𝑧| ≤ 𝑇 if and only if |ℎ(𝑧)| ≤ 𝐵for all 𝑧 ∈ ℂ with |𝑧| = 𝑇 .
L112.title: Maximum modulus principle

L113.statement: Let 𝑅, 𝑀 > 0. Suppose 𝑓(𝑧)is an analytic function on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 satisfying ℜ(𝑓(𝑧)) ≤ 𝑀. Then 2𝑀 − 𝑓(𝑧) ≠ 0for all |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅.
L113.title: Denom nonzero

L114.statement: Let 𝑅, 𝑀 > 0. Suppose 𝑓(𝑧)is an analytic function on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 satisfying ℜ(𝑓(𝑧)) ≤ 𝑀. For any 𝑧 with |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅, we have |𝑓(𝑧)| |2𝑀−𝑓(𝑧)|≤ 1.
L114.title: Ratio bound

L115.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 . Let 𝑓 be analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 such that 𝑓(0) = 0. Then the function ℎ(𝑧) = 𝑓(𝑧)/𝑧is analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅.
L115.title: Removable zero

L116.statement: Let 𝑅 > 0 . If ℎ1(𝑧)and ℎ2(𝑧)are analytic for |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 and ℎ2(𝑧) ≠ 0for all |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅, then ℎ1(𝑧)/ℎ2(𝑧)is analytic for |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅.
L116.title: Quotient analytic

D117.statement: Let 𝑅, 𝑀 > 0 . Let 𝑓 be analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 such that 𝑓(0) = 0and suppose ℜ𝑓(𝑧) ≤ 𝑀for all |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅. Define the function 𝑓𝑀 (𝑧)for |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 as 𝑓𝑀 (𝑧) = 𝑓(𝑧)/𝑧 2𝑀 − 𝑓(𝑧).
D117.title: Modified function

L118.statement: The function 𝑓𝑀 (𝑧)from Definition 117 is analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅.
L118.title: g analytic

L119.statement: Let 𝑎, 𝑏 ∈ ℂ. If 𝑏 ≠ 0 then |𝑎/𝑏| = |𝑎|/|𝑏|.
L119.title: Quotient modulus

L120.statement: Let 𝑅, 𝑀 > 0. Let 𝑓 be analytic on |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅 such that 𝑓(0) = 0and suppose ℜ𝑓(𝑧) ≤ 𝑀for all |𝑧| ≤ 𝑅. We have |𝑓𝑀 (𝑧)| =|𝑓(𝑧)/𝑧| |2𝑀−𝑓(𝑧)|.
L120.title: g modulus
